name: Lost Wiki: Kozlovka
short_description: Explore a wikipedia-like database and solve a small town mystery in Eastern Europe. Step into the shoes of a journalist in the 90s as you sprawl through articles and uncover the secrets that have been laid in front of you.
detailed_description:
Step into the shoes of a journalist as you uncover a mystery of a small town in Eastern Europe. Deduce and complete puzzles as you explore and uncover more articles in a sprawling wikipedia-like database.

Connect the dots of these interconnected web of articles. Use your skills of deduction to figure out the mystery and report back to your anonymous source. By completing puzzles, you’ve already gained knowledge for your next conundrum.


Something is wrong with the town you’re investigating. Flags continue to show as you delve deeper into their database. Figure out the connections, and the story will be laid bare.
about_the_game:

Something is wrong with the town you’re investigating. Flags continue to show as you delve deeper into their database. Figure out the connections, and the story will be laid bare.

reviews.review_text: Enjoyable little puzzle game. Not too challenging and very well paced. For accomplished puzzlers it may feel a little straightforward but the story has satisfying closure. The music and overall sound design is perfect for creating some drama/tension and making you feel like a proper journalist uncovering haunting secrets. Just wish it was longer!
A bit short but solid mystery.  Had a good time.

We (my gf and I) thoroughly enjoyed this game. A solid couple hours to solve a fascinating mystery through the lens of an old PC, navigating a wikipedia-style ledger. 

The atmosphere, visual, and audio design was absolutely superb, and really added to the overall feel of the game. Especially the CRT filter and monochrome shading. You can tell it was made with the love and passion one comes to expect of indie games today.

In terms of improvements, the controls could use a touch, as it felt confusing to use the arrows at the top left of the wiki page, and we would have appreciated them to go back to the page linking them instead of the last page we visited. Furthermore, it would have been nice to be able to rearrange the flowchart as we see fit, to organise the pages according to how we saw the evidence.

Overall, would 100% recommend to play the game, an excellent way to spend $5 for a fun 2-hour evening.

I unfortunately cannot recommend this game. I really admire the idea and the effort, but the experience is has flaws that I cannot overlook.
First off the wiki part falls off after like 5 questions, then you just have to answer samey questions from what you learned from it without ever adding new wiki articles etc. This was the main reason I bought this game, so I guess the name created a wrong expectation here. Wiki also gives me the idea that I will have more less useful information to look trough Second thing is some of the emails felt as a bit of a guess, like the one where you say that [spoiler]90s disappearances happened in the forest[/spoiler]. Forest was never explicitly or implicitly connected to the disappearances in the articles available at that moment. A lot of the mysteries seem to be solved through very circumstantial evidence and guesses, especially before we get to [spoiler]all the photos of letters[/spoiler], instead of adding new proof with wiki articles etc. Third thing is that the program that lets us view older versions of articles was pretty clunky to use, I would rather prefer that its activation would automatically make all the articles show all the information without the need to go trough this screen multiple times.
The story itself is very much okay, nothing too exciting. Since it talks a lot about one family, it gets very confusing when most characters have the same surname while being sisters/brother/fathers/mothers to each other.
I would love to see more in this genre, but hoping to have a more intricate wiki to look trough.
A fun little self contained database exploration mystery much like the root trees are dead. For 4 and a half dollars its quiet good!!
